question: What evidence supports preemptive TEVAR for uncomplicated acute or subacute type B aortic dissection?
source_access_type: Original publisher PDF held in private local source workspace
relevant_sections: Progression of Disease and Aorta-Specific Events

study_characteristics.design: Long-term intention-to-treat follow-up of the previously randomized INSTEAD cohort
study_characteristics.population: 140 stable survivors of uncomplicated type B aortic dissection originally randomized 2 to 52 weeks after onset
study_characteristics.intervention: Optimal medical therapy plus elective TEVAR (n=72 randomized)

source_identity_validation.metadata_warning: Core source identity matched by DOI, PMID, title, journal, and year. The printed PDF byline and indexed metadata differ regarding Sievers HH. This difference does not affect source identity; no authorship-credit determination is made.

extracted_claims.claim_text: INSTEAD-XL reports extended follow-up of the same 140-patient randomized INSTEAD cohort rather than a new randomization.
extracted_claims.exact_supporting_text: A total of 140 patients with stable type B aortic dissection previously randomized
extracted_claims.source_location_note: The Methods section identifies this as retrospective long-term analysis of the prior randomized cohort.
extracted_claims.confidence: High for extraction; not specialist reviewed

extracted_claims.claim_text: Five-year all-cause mortality was numerically lower with TEVAR but was not statistically significant in the overall analysis.
extracted_claims.exact_supporting_text: all-cause mortality trended lower in patients randomized to TEVAR
extracted_claims.confidence: High for extraction; not specialist reviewed

extracted_claims.claim_text: Five-year aorta-specific mortality was lower in the TEVAR group in the reported intention-to-treat analysis.
extracted_claims.source_location_note: Comparator was 19.3±4.8% with OMT; reported P=0.045 in body text (P=0.04 in abstract).
extracted_claims.confidence: High for extraction; not specialist reviewed

extracted_claims.claim_text: The reported five-year composite disease-progression endpoint occurred less often after assignment to TEVAR.
extracted_claims.exact_supporting_text: cumulative freedom from this cluster end point was 53.9±6.1% with OMT alone and 73.0±5.3% with TEVAR
extracted_claims.section: Progression of Disease and Aorta-Specific Events
extracted_claims.source_location_note: Abstract reports progression risks 27.0% versus 46.1%, P=0.04.
extracted_claims.confidence: High for extraction; not specialist reviewed

extracted_claims.claim_text: At five years, complete thoracic false-lumen thrombosis and remodeling were more frequent after TEVAR than with medical therapy alone.
extracted_claims.exact_supporting_text: complete false lumen thrombosis was confirmed in 90.6%
extracted_claims.source_location_note: Table 5 reports remodeling 79.2% versus 10.0% and complete thrombosis 90.6% versus 22.0%.
extracted_claims.confidence: High for extraction; not specialist reviewed

extracted_claims.claim_text: The late landmark analyses are observational and may omit early events, retain confounding, and lose power.
extracted_claims.exact_supporting_text: with Landmark analysis, findings become observational
extracted_claims.source_location_note: The authors explicitly describe time-point sensitivity, unaccounted confounding, omitted early events, and loss of power.
extracted_claims.confidence: High for extraction; not specialist reviewed

outcome_data.result_text: 6.9% versus 19.3%; P=0.04 in abstract (P=0.045 in body)

outcome_data.page_or_location: Printed p. 407; PDF p. 1; abstract; freedom estimates on printed p. 411

outcome_data.population: Patients surviving/event-free to landmark as analyzed
outcome_data.page_or_location: Printed p. 407; PDF p. 1; abstract; Figure 2A
Printed pp. 413-414; PDF pp. 7-8; Tables 4 and 5

limitations: Landmark analyses become observational, may omit early events, and are susceptible to confounding, time-point selection, and loss of power.
The analysis follows a modest-sized cohort originally powered for the two-year trial, with crossovers and secondary procedures over time.
Benefit may not emerge within the expected lifetime of older or severely comorbid patients.
The study used an earlier-generation Talent device and was partly funded by its manufacturer.
contradictions: The overall five-year all-cause mortality comparison was not statistically significant, while aorta-specific and late landmark outcomes favored TEVAR.
reviewer_notes: Original-PDF extraction only. Identity accepted with a bibliographic warning about Sievers HH. No authorship-credit determination was attempted. No claim or outcome is citation verified or specialist validated.
